candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; preferred over duplicate total line.
Duplicate total revenue line with identical values; less preferred than the cleaner primary revenue label.
Explicitly non-revenue, so not appropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Used revenue lines as a priority list because labels may vary over time; 'Revenue' is preferred and 'Total Revenue' is a fallback.

candidates.why: Best broad cost-of-sales style line combining direct materials/services; most appropriate COGS proxy.

candidates.row_label: Purchases of materials, supplies and goods
candidates.why: Direct input cost but narrower than the broader 'Materials and Services' line.
Very small residual component, not the main COGS line.
Balance sheet inventory component, not an income statement expense.

variables.notes: Selected the broadest direct production/service cost line rather than narrower subcomponents.

candidates.why: Could be overhead in some businesses, but here it sits within 'Materials and Services', suggesting COGS/direct service costs rather than SG&A.
Clear operating overhead bucket and above EBIT; appropriate SG&A component.
Clear operating overhead/personnel cost bucket and above EBIT; appropriate SG&A component.
Potential SG&A component, but likely already embedded in employee expenses; selecting it separately risks double counting.
Capitalized amount, not period SG&A expense.
Dedicated R&D expense exists, but it appears in supplemental disclosure below EBIT, so do not include in XSGA per rule.
Balance sheet asset, not SG&A expense.
Balance sheet liability, not income statement SG&A expense.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A subtotal exists. Chose the best overhead buckets above EBIT. Excluded 'External Services' because it is nested under 'Materials and Services' and is more consistent with COGS/direct service costs. Excluded 'Share-Based Compensation' to avoid likely double counting within employee expenses. Did not include R&D because the R&D line appears as supplemental disclosure below EBIT.

candidates.why: Capitalized development spending, not the period R&D expense to use for XRD.
Dedicated R&D expense line; best match for XRD.
Balance sheet asset, not period expense.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance sheet contra-asset/amortization, not current R&D expense.
Includes both capitalized and expensed R&D, so less suitable than the pure expense line.

variables.notes: Selected the dedicated expense line first. Included the total R&D expenditure line as a fallback in case labeling changes, though it is less preferred because it includes capitalized R&D. Not included in XSGA because these R&D rows appear below EBIT in the preview.

candidates.why: Broad finance cost line; usable fallback if specific interest expense missing.
Best direct match for interest expense.
Specific interest subtype; narrower than general interest expense.
Specific subtype and appears empty; not preferred.
Broad non-interest finance cost bucket; not preferred for XINT.
Broad non-interest finance cost bucket; not preferred for XINT.
Residual finance bucket; not preferred for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental restatement of lease interest, not preferred versus primary interest expense line.

variables.notes: Used a priority list with the specific interest line first and the broader finance expense line as fallback.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet book equity.

candidates.row_label: I/S Net profit for the period attributable to equity holders of the parent
candidates.why: Income statement profit attribution, not equity.
Only a component of equity, not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to equity holders of the parent
candidates.why: Best match because it captures parent equity and excludes non-controlling interests.
Total equity including NCI, less preferred because MIB is mapped separately.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to equity holders of the parent

variables.notes: Preferred the parent-attributable equity line to avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement OCI attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Direct balance sheet minority interest/NCI line; best match.
Duplicate balance sheet minority interest label; acceptable fallback.

variables.notes: Used balance sheet NCI labels as a priority list; both appear to be duplicate labels for the same concept.
